assertions.evidence: response.md: markdown table present with columns Scenario, Shipping Type, Order Value, Destination, Cost?.

assertions.text: Express shipping is represented as at least two distinct rows or cases: one where destination is irrelevant (paid tier, order under £50) and one where UK is specified (free tier, order £50+)
assertions.evidence: response.md: 'Express, order under £50 | Express | under £50 | [blank] | £9.99' (destination blank — irrelevant) and 'Express, order £50+, UK destination | Express | £50 or over | UK | £0' (UK specified for free tier) and 'Express, order £50+, non-UK destination | Express | £50 or over | non-UK | £9.99' (non-UK pays full price). The preamble also explicitly explains the asymmetry: 'destination is irrelevant for the paid tier, but determines the outcome for the high-value tier.'

assertions.text: Standard shipping destination is blank or absent from the table — it is genuinely irrelevant and should not be filled with a dummy value
assertions.evidence: response.md: 'Standard shipping, any order | Standard | any | [blank] | £3.99' — destination cell is blank. Preamble also confirms: 'Standard shipping destination is genuinely irrelevant — blank.'

assertions.text: Overnight shipping rows specify UK and/or Ireland as destinations — not left blank, since availability depends on it
assertions.evidence: response.md: 'Overnight shipping, UK | Overnight | any | UK | £14.99' and 'Overnight shipping, Ireland | Overnight | any | Ireland | £14.99' — both destinations explicitly specified. An additional row handles 'Other' destinations with 'not available'.

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
